description: Eval baseline for /charter, the first parent skill in the shirabe parent-skill pattern. Scenarios 1-6 (baseline-*) are the canonical shared eval baseline per Design Decision 4 in docs/designs/DESIGN-shirabe-progression-authoring.md; future parent skills (/scope, /work-on) copy-and-adapt these scenarios verbatim into their own evals.json until a future eval-format $ref mechanism mechanically retrofits the baseline. Scenarios 7-11 (us-*) cover /charter-specific PRD User Stories US-1, US-2, US-3a, US-3b, and US-4. Baseline scenarios use the 'baseline-' name prefix and appear first in the evals array; user-story scenarios use the 'us-' name prefix and follow.

evals.name: baseline-slug-rejection
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 validates the topic slug 'MyTopic' against the pattern-level regex ^[a-z0-9-]+$ and rejects it because of the uppercase letters, surfacing a clear error message that names the violated pattern. No state file is created. Other invocations such as /charter my_topic (underscore), /charter my.topic (dot), and /charter "Hello World" (whitespace and uppercase) are rejected the same way. Phase 0 validates $ARGUMENTS as provided byte-for-byte against the regex; there is no normalization, no derivation, and no "best effort" massaging.
expectations: Plan validates the topic slug 'MyTopic' against the regex ^[a-z0-9-]+$ at Phase 0 and rejects it with a clear error naming the violated pattern
Plan rejects /charter my_topic at Phase 0 for the underscore violation, /charter my.topic for the dot violation, and /charter "Hello World" for the whitespace and uppercase violation
Plan applies validation byte-for-byte against $ARGUMENTS with no normalization, no derivation, no auto-substitution into a conforming slug
Plan does NOT create wip/charter_<topic>_state.md on rejection; Phase 0 stops at the regex check
Plan names the violation category in the rejection error message (uppercase, underscore, dot, whitespace) so the author knows what to fix before re-invoking /charter

evals.name: baseline-malformed-state
evals.expected_output: wip/charter_test-topic_state.md exists but is malformed (unparseable YAML, missing required field for the recorded phase_pointer, or invalid exit / decision_record_sub_shape combination). The resume ladder fires row 1, surfaces a hard error naming the specific malformation, and offers Discard as the recovery path. /charter MUST NOT silently fall through to Phase 0 (row 10); malformed state is a contract violation surface, not a missing-state surface.
expectations: Plan detects the malformed state file at row 1 of the resume ladder and surfaces a hard error naming the specific malformation (e.g., unparseable YAML, missing required field, invalid exit / decision_record_sub_shape combo)
Plan offers Discard as the recovery path on row 1; the author confirms before the state file is removed
Plan does NOT silently fall through to row 10 (Phase 0 start fresh) when the state file is malformed; row 1 is terminal
Plan does not absorb or paraphrase the malformation; the error message names what is wrong so the author can decide between repair-by-hand or Discard

evals.name: baseline-child-internals-isolation
evals.expected_output: On resume against an existing chain state, /charter reads only the three permitted sources: child doc frontmatter status: at the published path, the child doc git blob hash (via read-only git hash-object), and /charter's own state file. /charter does NOT read child internal phase pointers, does NOT read wip/research/<child>_<topic>_phase<N>_*.md files, does NOT read any child wip/ intermediate beyond the named partial-run-detection patterns wip/strategy_<topic>_discover.md and wip/vision_<topic>_scope.md (rows 7-8 of the resume ladder). Manual-review verified by code-path inspection per AC20b.
expectations: Plan reads child doc frontmatter status: from the published path (docs/strategies/STRATEGY-<topic>.md, docs/visions/VISION-<topic>.md, docs/roadmaps/ROADMAP-<topic>.md) only — never from child internal state
Plan computes child doc git blob hash via read-only git hash-object against the published path — without git hash-object -w and without writes to the child doc or to git history

Plan does NOT read wip/research/<child>_<topic>_phase<N>_*.md files, child internal phase pointers, or any child wip/ intermediate beyond rows 7-8 named patterns (wip/strategy_<topic>_discover.md, wip/vision_<topic>_scope.md)
Plan reads /charter's own state file at wip/charter_<topic>_state.md but no other child-private state, log files, comment threads, or CI output
Plan binds child internals isolation to the R14-widened rule documented in references/parent-skill-child-inspection.md (citation in code-path inspection)

evals.name: baseline-visibility-default
evals.expected_output: CLAUDE.md is missing the ## Repo Visibility: header. Phase 1 visibility detection defaults to Private and emits a warning whose body contains the literal phrase "Default to Private if unknown". The warning prose names the missing ## Repo Visibility: header explicitly so the author knows what file to edit and what content to add. The default-Private behavior is conservative: a public repo that forgets to declare visibility gets the more-restrictive treatment.
expectations: Plan reads CLAUDE.md (or CLAUDE.local.md when present) and looks for a line matching ## Repo Visibility: (Public|Private)
Plan defaults to Private when the ## Repo Visibility: header is absent or its value is not Public or Private
Plan emits a warning whose body contains the literal phrase "Default to Private if unknown" followed by the rationale "restricting is easier to undo than oversharing"
Plan names the missing ## Repo Visibility: header explicitly in the warning so the author knows what to add to CLAUDE.md
Plan records the resolved visibility for the rest of the /charter run; the conservative default is documented per R12

evals.name: baseline-team-lead-discipline-loop-ordering
evals.expected_output: The Team-Lead Operating Discipline section in references/parent-skill-pattern.md (invariant I-7) documents a strict priority ordering for the team-lead's sleep-check-nudge loop: filesystem evidence (priority 1) MUST be checked before inbox processing (priority 2), which MUST be checked before sending a nudge (priority 3). The priority ordering is stated as STRICT, not advisory — a team-lead that consults the inbox before checking filesystem evidence misses durable terminal-artifact PASS that the peer has already produced; a team-lead that nudges before reading the inbox produces noise. /charter SKILL.md cites the discipline by path and by invariant ID I-7 from its Workflow Phases prelude, naming the implementation-pass task class (120s window / 10-cycle patience budget) as the binding for each child invocation.
expectations: Plan verifies that references/parent-skill-pattern.md Team-Lead Operating Discipline section documents priority 1 as filesystem evidence (artifact presence, git log for new commits since dispatch, wip/ growth)
Plan verifies that priority 2 is inbox processing of structured PASS/FAIL/PROGRESS verdicts; idle pings do NOT count as inbox messages and MUST NOT reset the patience budget
Plan verifies that priority 3 is the nudge, which MUST contain directly-executable instructions (what artifact, where, what verdict to reply with); generic nudges are forbidden

Plan verifies that the priority ordering is documented as STRICT (not advisory) — filesystem evidence BEFORE inbox messages BEFORE nudges, with the rationale that out-of-order checks lose evidence or produce noise

Plan verifies that /charter SKILL.md cites the discipline by path (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/references/parent-skill-pattern.md) and by invariant ID (I-7), naming the implementation-pass task class (120s window / 10-cycle patience budget) for each child invocation

evals.name: baseline-default-option-wording
evals.expected_output: An Accepted (or Active) STRATEGY exists at docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md. /charter's resume ladder fires row 5 and surfaces the entry-router prompt against the existing upstream. The prompt MUST contain the literal substring "Re-evaluate / Revise / Bail" (case-insensitive components allowed). The prompt MUST NOT contain the literal substring "Continue / Start fresh" — that vocabulary belongs to /strategy's status-aware re-entry and would hijack /charter's flow. The negative substring assertion is load-bearing: it prevents the PRD US-2 "Do you want to revise?" default from destroying the discipline-vs-artifact decoupling thesis the three-exit contract operationalizes. The three options Re-evaluate, Revise, and Bail are co-equal with no leading question; default-option wording is part of /charter's contract surface, not a UX detail.
expectations: Plan ensures the entry-router prompt against an existing Accepted/Active STRATEGY contains the literal substring "Re-evaluate / Revise / Bail" (case-insensitive components allowed)
Plan ensures the entry-router prompt MUST NOT contain the literal substring "Continue / Start fresh" — that vocabulary belongs to /strategy and would hijack /charter's flow
Plan ensures the negative substring assertion is documented as load-bearing — it prevents the "Do you want to revise?" default from destroying the discipline-vs-artifact decoupling thesis

Plan ensures the three options Re-evaluate, Revise, and Bail are presented as co-equal with no leading question; default-option wording is part of the contract surface per the pattern reference's Required SKILL.md Structural Elements section

evals.name: us-1-cold-standalone-full-run
evals.expected_output: Cold-start invocation against a fresh worktree under public-repo visibility: no wip/charter_test-topic_state.md exists, no docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md exists, no upstream artifacts. /charter walks Phase 1 discovery, surfaces the thesis-shift question but decides not to invoke /vision (no positive signal), omits the gated feeder per the public-repo degenerate-silence rule, invokes /strategy per R6, and conditionally invokes /roadmap when the produced STRATEGY's Building Blocks gate passes (3+ blocks AND a non-empty Coordination Dependencies entry). At chain finalization, /charter invokes shirabe validate --format json --visibility=public against the Draft STRATEGY, parses the shirabe-validate/v1 envelope, and proceeds on a clean (exit 0) result before declaring full-run success. State file: exit: full-run, exit_artifacts lists docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md (and docs/roadmaps/ROADMAP-test-topic.md when /roadmap ran).
expectations: Plan starts from Phase 0 (fresh worktree; no state file, no upstream artifacts) and validates the test-topic slug against ^[a-z0-9-]+$

Plan walks Phase 1 discovery, surfaces the literal question "Is the long-term thesis shifting, or is this an operational layer below it?", and decides not to invoke /vision when no positive signal fires
Plan does not invoke the gated feeder under public-repo visibility — the chain-proposal output omits any reference to it per the degenerate-silence rule
Plan invokes /strategy per R6 with one of three valid upstream shapes (freeform topic, VISION path, or PRD path); plan does NOT pass a STRATEGY path

Plan conditionally invokes /roadmap when the produced STRATEGY has 3+ Building Blocks AND a non-empty Coordination Dependencies entry; plan skips /roadmap and completes at STRATEGY-only full-run when the shape gates fail

Plan invokes shirabe validate --format json --visibility=public as a sub-process against the Draft STRATEGY before declaring full-run success, parses the shirabe-validate/v1 envelope, and branches on the multi-level exit code: clean (0) proceeds; violations (2) block finalization and surface each error-severity finding readably as message (file:line) — the message already embeds the check code, so it is not prepended again — rather than verbatim annotation text; tool-error (1) halts as a tool failure distinct from a content violation

Plan writes state file with exit: full-run; exit_artifacts contains exactly one entry (STRATEGY path with status Draft) for STRATEGY-only chains; exactly two entries (STRATEGY + ROADMAP, each with status Draft) for STRATEGY + ROADMAP chains

Plan keeps conditional fields decision_record_sub_shape, referenced_strategy, discard_commit_sha, rejection_rationale, triggering_child, and partial_phase_reached ABSENT from the state file per R9 conditional-field gating

evals.name: us-2-re-evaluation
evals.expected_output: An Accepted STRATEGY exists at docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md (no wip/charter_test-topic_state.md). /charter's resume ladder fires row 5 against the Accepted upstream and surfaces an entry prompt containing the literal substrings "Re-evaluate", "Revise", and "Bail" (case-insensitive). The prompt MUST NOT contain the substring "Continue / Start fresh" — that vocabulary belongs to /strategy and would hijack /charter's flow. The author selects Re-evaluate; /charter walks the existing STRATEGY's Bet-Specific Falsifiability claims, finds all claims still hold, and writes a Decision Record at docs/decisions/DECISION-strategy-test-topic-re-evaluation-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md citing the existing STRATEGY by path. The child /strategy is NOT invoked. State file: exit: re-evaluation, decision_record_sub_shape: re-evaluation, referenced_strategy: docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md.
expectations: Plan detects the Accepted STRATEGY at docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md and fires resume-ladder row 5 with the entry prompt offering Re-evaluate / Revise / Bail (case-insensitive substrings present)
Plan ensures the row-5 prompt does NOT contain the substring "Continue / Start fresh" — that is /strategy's status-aware re-entry vocabulary, not /charter's
Plan rejects the PRD US-2 "Do you want to revise?" default as the entry prompt; the three options are co-equal with no leading question
Plan walks the existing STRATEGY's Bet-Specific Falsifiability claims when Re-evaluate is selected and concludes whether all claims still hold

Plan writes the re-evaluation Decision Record at docs/decisions/DECISION-strategy-test-topic-re-evaluation-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md with the five ADR-style sections Status, Context, Decision, Options Considered, and Consequences
Plan ensures the Decision Record's Options Considered section names both "revise the STRATEGY" and "force-abandon and rewrite" as rejected alternatives
Plan ensures the Decision Record's frontmatter contains the three fields status, decision, and rationale per AC25; status defaults to Accepted
Plan does NOT invoke /strategy on the Re-evaluate path; /charter authors the Decision Record directly

Plan writes state file with exit: re-evaluation, decision_record_sub_shape: re-evaluation, and referenced_strategy: docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md; the conditional fields discard_commit_sha, rejection_rationale, triggering_child, and partial_phase_reached are ABSENT

evals.name: us-3a-rejection-sub-shape
evals.expected_output: Cold start. /charter invokes /strategy per R6; /strategy reaches Phase 5 and the author selects Reject as a deliberate finalization judgment (NOT a bail). /strategy runs git rm docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md, cleans up wip/strategy_test-topic_*.md, and commits the discard with message docs(strategy): discard STRATEGY draft for test-topic. Control returns to /charter; /charter captures the discard commit SHA via read-only git log (no git writes from /charter). /charter writes a Decision Record at docs/decisions/DECISION-strategy-test-topic-rejection-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md citing the discard commit SHA and the author's rejection rationale. State file: exit: re-evaluation, decision_record_sub_shape: rejection, discard_commit_sha: <sha>, rejection_rationale: <text>.
expectations: Plan invokes /strategy per R6 and the chain reaches /strategy's Phase 5 finalization phase

Plan recognizes /strategy Phase 5 Reject as /strategy's deliberate finalization judgment routed into the rejection sub-shape of Exit 2 — NOT routed as a bail (which would produce Exit 3 abandonment-forced)

Plan respects that /strategy runs git rm docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md, cleans wip/strategy_test-topic_*.md, and commits the discard; /charter issues NO git writes during the rejection orchestration
Plan captures the discard commit SHA via read-only git log after /strategy returns control to /charter

Plan writes the rejection Decision Record at docs/decisions/DECISION-strategy-test-topic-rejection-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md with the five ADR-style sections (Status, Context, Decision, Options Considered, Consequences) per AC25
Plan ensures the rejection Decision Record's Context section references the discard commit SHA so a future reader can navigate to the git history
Plan ensures the rejection Decision Record's Options Considered section names both "accept the Draft" and "revise instead of reject" as rejected alternatives
Plan ensures the Decision Record's frontmatter contains status, decision, and rationale per AC25

Plan writes state file with exit: re-evaluation, decision_record_sub_shape: rejection, discard_commit_sha (captured from git log), rejection_rationale (free-text from the author); conditional fields referenced_strategy, triggering_child, and partial_phase_reached are ABSENT

evals.name: us-3b-abandonment-forced
evals.expected_output: wip/charter_test-topic_state.md exists with phase_pointer past Phase 0, exit: UNSET, and last_updated more than 7 days old. /charter's resume ladder fires row 4 (state file stale) and surfaces the three-option prompt Resume / Force-materialize / Discard. The author selects Force-materialize. /charter resolves the most-recently-running child via the R8 tie-break (step 1: last entry in chain_ran; step 2: first planned_chain entry with a non-empty wip/ intermediate; step 3: clean-cancel when neither step resolves) and force-materializes that child's intermediate as a schema-compliant Draft artifact. The Draft contains an HTML-comment marker <!-- charter-status-block: abandonment-forced; triggering-child: <name>; partial-phase-reached: <phase>; chain-started: <ISO-8601 timestamp> --> inside the artifact's existing Status section, NOT in a new section that would invalidate the host artifact-type's schema validator. State file: exit: abandonment-forced, triggering_child: <child-name>, partial_phase_reached: <phase-name>.
expectations: Plan detects last_updated >= 7 days old at row 4 of the resume ladder and surfaces the three-option prompt Resume / Force-materialize / Discard; the prompt fires on every invocation while the state remains stale

Plan routes Force-materialize into Exit 3 abandonment-forced and resolves the triggering child via the R8 three-step tie-break (chain_ran last entry; then planned_chain first entry with non-empty wip/; then clean-cancel fallthrough)

Plan emits the HTML-comment marker <!-- charter-status-block: abandonment-forced; triggering-child: <name>; partial-phase-reached: <phase>; chain-started: <ISO-8601 timestamp> --> inside the force-materialized artifact's existing Status section, NOT in a new required section

Plan keeps the host artifact type's schema validator passing by using HTML-comment syntax (the marker is invisible to schema validation; greppable as charter-status-block: for tools and human readers)

Plan writes state file with exit: abandonment-forced, triggering_child set to the resolved child name, partial_phase_reached set to the phase pointer the triggering child had reached, chain_completed set to the abandonment timestamp
Plan keeps decision_record_sub_shape ABSENT from the state file per R9 conditional-field gating; abandonment-forced exits do not carry a Decision Record sub-shape

Plan ensures the marker uses hyphenated field names (triggering-child, partial-phase-reached, chain-started) per the marker convention; this differs from the underscored YAML state-schema field names (triggering_child, partial_phase_reached, chain_started) by design
Plan accepts STRATEGY, VISION, or ROADMAP as the host artifact type for the force-materialized Draft, per the R8 tie-break resolution

evals.name: us-4-manual-fallback-reviewer-redirect
evals.expected_output: Pre-populated: docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md at status Accepted, and wip/charter_test-topic_state.md whose child_snapshots.strategy records path: docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md plus status: Accepted plus content_hash matching the STRATEGY's git hash-object value at snapshot time. A reviewer invokes /strategy docs/strategies/STRATEGY-test-topic.md directly outside /charter; /strategy produces a revised Draft, flipping the frontmatter status: from Accepted to Draft and rewriting the body. The live git hash-object now differs from the snapshot's content_hash. /charter does NOT interfere with the manual /strategy invocation: no warning, no state-file write, no block. On the next /charter test-topic invocation, the resume ladder runs drift detection (dual check: status OR content_hash differs from the snapshot) and surfaces the three-option staleness prompt Re-run / Accept / Proceed without.
expectations: Plan respects R13 manual-fallback non-interference: when the reviewer invokes /strategy directly outside /charter, /charter emits no warning, performs no state-file write against the manual invocation, and does NOT block direct child invocation

Plan reads the child doc's frontmatter status: and computes the child doc's git hash-object (read-only) at the published path on resume, then compares both against the recorded child_snapshots.strategy entry (path + status + content_hash)

Plan fires drift detection when EITHER the live status differs from the snapshot status OR the live content_hash (blob hash) differs from the snapshot content_hash — both directions of the dual check are load-bearing
Plan surfaces the three-option staleness prompt naming Re-run (re-invoke the affected child), Accept (record acknowledgment and proceed), and Proceed without (skip the affected child for this chain)

Plan reads only the durable externally-visible status surface (frontmatter status: + git blob hash) per the R14-widened rule; the plan does NOT read child internals, wip/research/<child>_*.md, or any other child-private state to detect drift
Plan does NOT act on drift unilaterally; the staleness prompt surfaces /charter's three options and the author chooses the path
